children.label: A
children.text: the purposes of this chapter; and

children.label: B
children.text: the purposes for which the funds, equipment, or supplies were made available to the Federal agency.

children.label: A
children.text: accept from any source, including Federal and State agencies, funds, equipment, or supplies that are available for the construction or operation of health care or sanitation facilities; and

children.label: B
children.text: use those funds, equipment, and supplies to plan, design,, construct, and operate health care or sanitation facilities for Indians, including pursuant to a contract or compact under the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ( et seq.).125 U.S.C. 4502So in original.1See References in Text note below.2

children.heading: Effect of receipt
children.label: 3 Effect of receipt
children.text: Receipt of funds by the Secretary under this subsection shall not affect any priority established under .section 1631 of this title
the purposes of this chapter; and
the purposes for which the funds were appropriated or otherwise provided.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Service, shall establish, by regulation, standards for the planning, design, construction, and operation of health care or sanitation facilities serving Indians under this chapter.

children.heading: Other regulations
children.label: 2 Other regulations
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any other applicable regulations of the Department shall apply in carrying out projects using funds transferred under this section.

children.heading: Definition of sanitation facility
children.label: d Definition of sanitation facility
children.text: In this section, the term “sanitation facility” means a safe and adequate water supply system, sanitary sewage disposal system, or sanitary solid waste system (including all related equipment and support infrastructure).
